181
IBD
Classifications (location
and evolving phenotype)
Crohn's disease
In pediatric CD, the ileo-colic location is the most
frequent (42).
The PCDAI (Pediatric Crohn Disease Activity
Index) is a modification of the CDAI (Crohn Disease Activity
Index), which incorporates data specific to growth
and biological parameters (table 4).
In children, CD generally progresses more
rapidly and aggressively than in adults. An American
study of 276 pediatric patients found that disease
extension was observed in nearly 40%
of children within the first two years after
diagnosis. In addition, complicated phenotypes (B2:
stricturing, B3: fistulizing) affected 24% of cases
after four years, compared with only 9% at the
time of diagnosis (9). These phenotypic changes occur
mostly within five years of disease onset.
Table 2: pediatric forms of CD at diagnosis (41)
Category
Typical features
Non-specific features
Macroscopic
-	Ulcerative aphthous lesions of the mucosa
-	Linear ulcers or
